chunks.title: Strings Slicing and Operations on Strings in Python
chunks.text: In 100 days of code, everyone's tempo is going to be very high  And today's topic is how to do string slicing  Remember we read about string, we saw how good this data type is  How useful data type is, you guys make any project  You will see the string being used in some way or the other

chunks.title: Strings Slicing and Operations on Strings in Python
chunks.text: Today I will tell you about string slicing  And I will tell you some more interesting things about string  Which will make it easier for you to play with string  Let's go to the computer screen and let's get started  Intro Music

chunks.title: Strings Slicing and Operations on Strings in Python
chunks.text: So today we are going to see again  Our old topic which was strings  So first of all I will open this wrapper in my strings  And today we are going to see how string slicing is done  Let's say we have a string named Harry

chunks.title: Strings Slicing and Operations on Strings in Python
chunks.text: And it is made of 5 characters H, A, R, R, Y  Let's say you want to access from R to Y  Or if you don't like this example  Let's say I gave you a string which has 5 names  Let me quickly come to my wrapper and explain to you what I am talking about

chunks.title: Strings Slicing and Operations on Strings in Python
chunks.text: Let's say I have a name  I will give you names here  H, A, R, R, Y, S, H, U, B, H, A, M  In fact I will name it  Let's say I have comma separated names

chunks.title: Strings Slicing and Operations on Strings in Python
chunks.text: I want only Harry  So I can do something like this  Print names  And after this I want to take 5 characters from the beginning  How will I take the initial 5 characters

chunks.title: Strings Slicing and Operations on Strings in Python
chunks.text: First of all I will specify the starting index  And after this I will specify the ending index  I will say that I start from the starting index  Index starts from 0 by the way  Whenever we count something in our life, we start from 1

chunks.title: Strings Slicing and Operations on Strings in Python
chunks.text: If we count the fingers of the hand, then we start from 1, 2, 3, 4, 5  Whatever we do, the counting starts from 1  In programming, whatever you do, starting from 0 is not 1  Because most of the programming languages start from 0  This is clear now

chunks.title: Strings Slicing and Operations on Strings in Python
chunks.text: Now what I did is I started counting from 0  And what I will do here is I will write 6  Why did I write 6, I want 5 characters from the beginning  So why did I write 6  I will write 5, so I will get 5 characters

chunks.title: Strings Slicing and Operations on Strings in Python
chunks.text: So I will get from 0 to 4  If you hover like this  So see here this replete tells you a lot  But now leaving all these things, I will tell you how it works  This is from 0 to n-1

chunks.title: Strings Slicing and Operations on Strings in Python
chunks.text: The value of n is 5 here  It prints you the indices  Like it did 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5  It did not do 5, this thing is to be remembered  Now I have written such an interesting tutorial for you

chunks.title: Strings Slicing and Operations on Strings in Python
chunks.text: See here, length of a string  We can find the length of a string using length function  Before all this, I would like to tell you  If you want to find the length of any string  Like this string named names

chunks.title: Strings Slicing and Operations on Strings in Python
chunks.text: I want the length of this  So I will use length function and put the string in it  13 came, count it  2, 2, 4, 2, 6, 2, 8, 2, 10, 2, 12, and 1, 13  13 characters came here

chunks.title: Strings Slicing and Operations on Strings in Python
chunks.text: So you can find the length like this  I have given an example here, we can try it out  Mango is a 5 character word  5 letter word  So we have 5 letters in mango

chunks.title: Strings Slicing and Operations on Strings in Python
chunks.text: So mango is a 5 letter word  Now string is basically an array  It is a sequence of characters  We saw this in the last video  We can access any element of this

chunks.title: Strings Slicing and Operations on Strings in Python
chunks.text: Now see here, I will explain slicing to you  Leave this now and listen to me  I have fruit is equal to mango  I have printed len1 here  Or mango len

chunks.title: Strings Slicing and Operations on Strings in Python
chunks.text: I can keep anything, I can keep anything  I have kept mango len  So I will get 5 prints here  Now I want to get the initial two characters of fruit  So I will write fruit

chunks.title: Strings Slicing and Operations on Strings in Python
chunks.text: And after that I will write print fruit  And I will put this square bracket notation  Make sure you don't use parenthesis  We use parenthesis for sections  But for slicing we use square brackets

chunks.title: Strings Slicing and Operations on Strings in Python
chunks.text: Square brackets are on your keyboard  They are on the right side of P  You can see it in your keyboard  And from 0 to  Let's say I write up to 4

chunks.title: Strings Slicing and Operations on Strings in Python
chunks.text: So I will get 4 characters at the beginning  I am basically saying this line  Give zeroth index, first index  Give second index  Give third index

chunks.title: Strings Slicing and Operations on Strings in Python
chunks.text: You will not see the fourth index  You will not see the fourth index  You will see only 0 to 3  0, 1, 2, 3, 4 characters  I will run and show you

chunks.title: Strings Slicing and Operations on Strings in Python
chunks.text: M, A, N, G will be the output  What will happen if I don't put 0  I can skip 0  If I don't put 0  Then what will Python do

chunks.title: Strings Slicing and Operations on Strings in Python
chunks.text: It will automatically put 0 here  If I skip it, this is a very good feature of Python  Now what will happen if I want to start from 1  I want from index 1  So I will write from 1 to 4

chunks.title: Strings Slicing and Operations on Strings in Python
chunks.text: So I will get A, N and G  Now see here I got A, N and G  But if I write something like this  Index and colon and 4  So automatically

chunks.title: Strings Slicing and Operations on Strings in Python
chunks.text: Python is so smart  It will put 0  So it will put 0 here  And you will get mang again  If I remove 4 here

chunks.title: Strings Slicing and Operations on Strings in Python
chunks.text: So what will Python do  It will put length of fruit  What is length of fruit?  It is 5, so it will put 5  So if I don't put 5

chunks.title: Strings Slicing and Operations on Strings in Python
chunks.text: So I am getting mango  And if I put 5, I will get mango  So this is how slicing works  Now about slicing  I want to tell you one more thing

chunks.title: Strings Slicing and Operations on Strings in Python
chunks.text: Negative slicing  If I put minus 1 here  I will put 0 here  So 0 will come automatically  And if I write minus 3 here

chunks.title: Strings Slicing and Operations on Strings in Python
chunks.text: What will happen  I will tell you why it came  It is M, A output  Why it came  So let's understand how Python interprets this

chunks.title: Strings Slicing and Operations on Strings in Python
chunks.text: The minus 3  Here len of  Fruit, this is what Python  Interpreter puts automatically  Length of fruit, how much is len of fruit?

chunks.title: Strings Slicing and Operations on Strings in Python
chunks.text: It is 5, we saw it is 5  Len of fruit is 5  And 5 minus 3 is 2  So 0 to 2 means  0 M

chunks.title: Strings Slicing and Operations on Strings in Python
chunks.text: And then 1 means A  0 is on index M  A is on first index  So M is printed  I will tell you for clarity

chunks.title: Strings Slicing and Operations on Strings in Python
chunks.text: If I write this  Or this  It will be the same  Python automatically puts len of fruit  What about negative slicing

chunks.title: Strings Slicing and Operations on Strings in Python
chunks.text: Here, if I put minus 1  What will be printed  Wait a minute  I will write it in a new line  So you don't get confused

chunks.title: Strings Slicing and Operations on Strings in Python
chunks.text: If I don't write anything here  Because Python automatically puts len of fruit  For negative slicing  And if I put minus 1  What will happen

chunks.title: Strings Slicing and Operations on Strings in Python
chunks.text: Then you see  I will comment it  So you can see what is being printed  If I do it like this  Minus 1

chunks.title: Strings Slicing and Operations on Strings in Python
chunks.text: 5 is printed and nothing is printed  Now let's understand what is this  Here I wrote len of fruit minus 3  And here I wrote minus 1  So what will happen

chunks.title: Strings Slicing and Operations on Strings in Python
chunks.text: Python interpreter will automatically put len of fruit  So python interpreter  Will interpret it like this  Len of fruit is 5  So 5 minus 1 is 4

chunks.title: Strings Slicing and Operations on Strings in Python
chunks.text: And 5 minus 3 is 2  So there is no sense of 4 to 2  However, if I do it in reverse  That is, I put minus 3 here  And here I put minus 1

chunks.title: Strings Slicing and Operations on Strings in Python
chunks.text: Then what will happen  And I don't even need to put len of fruit  As I was telling you  Python automatically puts len of fruit  If I run it

chunks.title: Strings Slicing and Operations on Strings in Python
chunks.text: Then you will see that ng is coming  How did this ng come, let's decode it  First of all, python will put  Len of fruit minus 3  Which is 5 minus 3 is 2

chunks.title: Strings Slicing and Operations on Strings in Python
chunks.text: So here it will be 2  And here it will be 4  So it will start from the second index  And will go to the third  Remember that python starts from here

chunks.title: Strings Slicing and Operations on Strings in Python
chunks.text: And it goes to minus 1  So 0, 1, 2 means n will come  Then 3 will also come, which means g will come  And the fourth index will be ignored  I told you

chunks.title: Strings Slicing and Operations on Strings in Python
chunks.text: Including this but not including this  And I would like to write this  I would like to write this  Including 0 but not 4  Including 0 but not 4

chunks.title: Strings Slicing and Operations on Strings in Python
chunks.text: In fact, I will write the same thing here  So that you don't think  If it is 0, then there is no special case  No, there is no special case of 0  In which I will paste you here

chunks.title: Strings Slicing and Operations on Strings in Python
chunks.text: And it will be the same for 1  So you can see  If you do 0 to 4, then including 0  But not 4, including 1  But not 4, that is, from 1 to 3

chunks.title: Strings Slicing and Operations on Strings in Python
chunks.text: And this is the index  That is, the number that starts counting from 0  If you start counting from 0  That is, instead of 0, we are at 1  Instead of 2, we are at n

chunks.title: Strings Slicing and Operations on Strings in Python
chunks.text: Instead of 3, we are at g  And instead of 4, we are at o  So this way string slicing can be done  All examples are given here  Now many people explain negative slicing in a different way

chunks.title: Strings Slicing and Operations on Strings in Python
chunks.text: They say, do the count upside down  But I think the best explanation is  Write the line of truth here  And that's about it, your work is done  Okay, now we saw
